title: Combinatorics on Words in String Amplitudes
speaker: Oliver Schlotterer

proof_stages.label: Motivation and roadmap for word combinatorics
proof_stages.summary: The talk frames shuffle products and the s-bracket as universal combinatorial tools for organizing multiparticle gauge and string amplitudes, with field-theory, tree-level string, integration, and loop-level aspects.

proof_stages.label: Words, shuffles, and KK reductions
proof_stages.summary: External legs are encoded as nonrepeating words, the shuffle product is defined recursively, and Kleiss-Kuijf relations are presented as the vanishing of pure shuffles for color-ordered Yang-Mills amplitudes and Parke-Taylor factors.

proof_stages.label: BCJ relations encoded by the s-bracket
proof_stages.summary: The Bern-Carrasco-Johansson relations are reformulated as the vanishing of amplitudes evaluated on an s-bracket of two nonempty words, with the bracket defined by deconcatenation sums in the quotient by pure shuffles.

proof_stages.label: Heterotic tree integrands and alpha-prime uplift
proof_stages.summary: Single-trace heterotic gluon amplitudes are written as moduli-space integrals with Parke-Taylor color dependence, so KK relations lift immediately and BCJ/s-bracket relations follow because the s-bracket insertion becomes a total derivative against the Koba-Nielsen factor.

proof_stages.label: KLT matrix and Lie structure from nested s-brackets
proof_stages.summary: The remaining antiholomorphic integrand is expanded using Yang-Mills trees, Parke-Taylor factors, and the field-theory KLT matrix, whose polynomial entries are generated by nested s-brackets and whose consistency rests on antisymmetry and Jacobi identities modulo shuffles.

proof_stages.label: Disk-integral reduction and interpolating deformation
proof_stages.summary: To compute alpha-prime expansions, sphere integrals are reduced to disk-integral data, and an auxiliary marked point with momentum is introduced to form deformed integrals that interpolate recursively between multiplicities n-1 and n.

proof_stages.label: KZ connection matrices computed by s-brackets
proof_stages.summary: Differentiating the deformed disk integrals with respect to the auxiliary point yields a Knizhnik-Zamolodchikov equation, and integration-by-parts reductions express the connection matrices E0 and E1 in closed form using s-bracket deconcatenations.

proof_stages.label: Associator solution and higher-genus outlook
proof_stages.summary: The KZ equation is solved by a shuffle-regularized path-ordered exponential, the Drinfeld/KZ associator generating multiple zeta values, and the talk concludes by explaining that shuffle mechanisms persist at loop level through Fay identities on higher-genus worldsheets.

examples.name: Shuffling decks of cards
examples.note: The speaker likens the shuffle product of two words to interleaving two ordered decks of cards, making the recursive definition visually concrete without affecting the argument.
examples.engagement_reason: uses shuffling decks of cards only as an intuition for the shuffle product, without carrying out a concrete shuffle calculation

examples.name: Four-point BCJ relation
examples.note: A specialization of the bulky BCJ/s-bracket formula to four gluons shows explicitly how Mandelstam coefficients relate two color orderings.
examples.engagement_reason: specializes the general BCJ/s-bracket relation to four points and explains the resulting Mandelstam-weighted relation between color-ordered Yang--Mills trees

passes.reason: minimal four-point BCJ case where the general s-bracket relation collapses to one Mandelstam-weighted identity
minimal nontrivial four-point BCJ identity among color-ordered trees, used illustratively rather than to refute a claim
lowest nontrivial BCJ relation, with the general s-bracket identity collapsed to a two-term Mandelstam relation
minimal four-point BCJ identity where the general deconcatenation sum collapses to a single Mandelstam-weighted relation
The four-point BCJ identity is the lowest nontrivial/base case of the general BCJ relations and is used illustratively.
the four-point BCJ identity is the minimal nontrivial collapsed case of the general n-point s-bracket relations
The four-point BCJ relation is the lowest nontrivial/base case of the general BCJ family, used illustratively rather than adversarially.
The four-point BCJ relation is presented as the simplest low-point/base-case instance of the general BCJ/s-bracket relations.
four-point specialization collapses the general BCJ/s-bracket sum to a single simple amplitude relation
the four-point BCJ identity is the minimal nontrivial instance of the general BCJ/s-bracket relations.
the four-point BCJ identity is the smallest nontrivial low-multiplicity instance used to unpack the general formula.
The four-point BCJ relation is the lowest nontrivial two-term base case of the general BCJ/s-bracket relations.

examples.name: Four-point Virasoro-Shapiro gamma ratio
examples.note: In response to a question about the functional dependence of the string amplitude, the speaker cites the four-point case as a ratio of gamma functions known as the Virasoro-Shapiro amplitude.
examples.engagement_reason: only states that at four points the heterotic/string amplitude involves a gamma-function ratio known as Virasoro--Shapiro and explicitly avoids discussing it further

passes.reason: low-point crossing-symmetric gamma-ratio cited to reject the idea that the full alpha-prime dependence is merely rational
four-point Virasoro-Shapiro amplitude collapses to a simple crossing-symmetric gamma ratio
lowest nontrivial closed-string four-point amplitude, reducing to the maximally crossing-symmetric Virasoro-Shapiro gamma ratio
four-point Virasoro-Shapiro factor is a low-multiplicity gamma-ratio with crossing symmetry, cited illustratively
The Virasoro-Shapiro gamma ratio is a fully crossing-symmetric four-point, hence minimal-multiplicity, closed-string amplitude example.
the Virasoro-Shapiro four-point factor is the low-multiplicity gamma-ratio case rather than a generic higher-point integral
The four-point Virasoro-Shapiro gamma ratio is the minimal nontrivial closed-string amplitude case, cited as a simple instance of the general functional dependence.
The Virasoro-Shapiro gamma ratio is cited as the elementary four-point closed-string case of the more complicated general amplitude.
crossing-symmetric four-point closed-string gamma ratio cited as a low-multiplicity special case
the Virasoro-Shapiro gamma ratio is the basic four-point closed-string amplitude at the low-multiplicity boundary.
the four-point Virasoro-Shapiro gamma ratio refutes the rational-function expectation and is the highly crossing-symmetric lowest-multiplicity closed-string case.
The Virasoro-Shapiro gamma ratio is cited as the simple four-point base case of the string-amplitude functional dependence.

examples.name: Open-string monodromy contrast
examples.note: Open-string gauge amplitudes are mentioned as a contrasting case where KK and BCJ relations are alpha-prime-deformed into monodromy relations, clarifying the specificity of the heterotic discussion.
examples.engagement_reason: contrasts open strings by saying their gauge-amplitude relations are alpha-prime-deformed monodromy relations, but does not analyze an open-string example

passes.reason: open-string monodromy relations are used as the contrasting case where the undeformed heterotic KK/BCJ uplift does not apply
open-string monodromy relations are invoked as a warning that the undeformed heterotic KK/BCJ relations do not generally carry over

open-string monodromy relations are used to show the undeformed heterotic KK/BCJ relations do not extend unchanged to open strings
Open-string monodromy relations form a systematic family but are cited to show exact heterotic KK/BCJ relations do not carry over unchanged.
open-string monodromy relations are used to show the undeformed heterotic KK/BCJ relations are not universal

examples.name: Four-point KZ matrices and Veneziano factor
examples.note: The two-by-two four-point connection matrices illustrate the general s-bracket construction, although the speaker stresses this is an over-elaborate way to recover the familiar Veneziano factor.
examples.engagement_reason: applies the KZ differential-equation setup at four points, writes the resulting two-by-two matrices, discusses setting p0 to zero, and relates their integration to the Veneziano gamma factor

passes.reason: four-point KZ example has only 2-by-2 matrices and collapses to the classical Veneziano beta factor
four-point KZ system is the smallest 2-by-2 case and merely reproduces the elementary Veneziano beta factor
four-point KZ system is the minimal two-by-two base case giving the simple Veneziano beta factor
four-point KZ example has only 2-by-2 matrices and reduces the machinery to the Veneziano beta factor
The two-by-two four-point KZ matrices giving the Veneziano factor are the simplest base case of the KZ-integral machinery.
the four-point KZ system reduces to small 2-by-2 matrices and the elementary Veneziano beta/gamma factor
The four-point KZ matrices and resulting Veneziano factor form the tiny 2-by-2 minimal case of the KZ-recursion method, presented as a sanity-check example.
The four-point KZ matrices and resulting Veneziano factor are used as the minimal toy computation before genuinely harder higher-point disk integrals.
four-point disk/KZ case collapses to 2x2 matrices and the beta-function Veneziano factor as the simplest check
the two-by-two four-point KZ example yielding the Veneziano factor is the minimal base case of the recursion.
the two-by-two four-point KZ example collapses to the basic Veneziano beta factor as a toy base case.
The four-point KZ matrices are the minimal 2-by-2 case and merely reproduce the elementary Veneziano factor.

examples.name: Five-point disk integrals
examples.note: The five-point case, where hypergeometric 3F2 functions and larger connection matrices appear, illustrates why a multiplicity-agnostic recursive method is useful.
examples.engagement_reason: uses five-point disk integrals as the first harder case, applies the KZ-matrix method to them, and displays a typical five-point E0 matrix rather than merely citing them

passes.reason: five points is the first disk-integral multiplicity beyond the Veneziano case where genuine hypergeometric complexity begins
five-point disk integrals are presented as the first threshold where hypergeometric 3F2 complexity appears
five-point disk integrals are the first threshold beyond Veneziano where hypergeometric 3F2-type complexity appears
five-point disk integrals are the first genuinely harder disk case beyond Veneziano, where hypergeometric complexity appears
Five-point disk integrals are the first genuinely harder multiparticle case where hypergeometric complexity begins.
five points is the first genuinely multiparticle disk-integral case where the hypergeometric complexity starts
Five-point disk integrals are the first genuinely harder case beyond the Veneziano four-point case, marking the threshold where the general method becomes useful.
Five-point disk integrals are highlighted as the first threshold where the integration problem becomes genuinely harder, requiring hypergeometric functions.
first disk-integral multiplicity beyond the Veneziano beta case where hypergeometric 3F2 complexity appears
five-point disk integrals are presented as the first genuinely harder multiparticle case beyond the four-point beta function.
